description: Search across a Notion workspace using the Cmd+K quick search. Returns matching pages, databases, and blocks.
author: stillwater

reasoning.research: Notion's workspace search is triggered by Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K on Mac) which opens a search modal. The modal has an input field with placeholder 'Search or jump to...' (selector: input[placeholder*='Search']). Results render as .notion-search-result items in a dropdown list. Each result has the page title, breadcrumb path, and icon. Clicking a result navigates to that page.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Trigger search modal via Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut (most reliable). Wait for search input: input[placeholder*='Search']. Type query with 80ms delay. Wait 2s for results to load. Extract results via .notion-search-result-item or similar container. Each result has title and path.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: Notion search is client-side with debounce — wait 1500ms after typing for full results. No bot detection on search. Valid session required to access workspace content.
reasoning.performance: Navigate 4s + shortcut 1s + type query 2s + wait results 2s + extract 1s = ~10s total.

query.description: Search query to find pages, databases, or blocks

workspace_url.description: Any Notion workspace URL to trigger search from

steps.description: Verify authenticated — abort with SESSION_EXPIRED if login page
Open Notion search modal via keyboard shortcut
Wait for search modal input to appear
Wait for search debounce and result rendering
Extract search results with titles and breadcrumb paths
Return search results as structured data

error_handling.session_expired: Detect notion.so/login redirect — run notion-oauth-login recipe first
error_handling.search_modal_not_open: Fallback: click search icon in sidebar if Ctrl+K fails
error_handling.no_results: Return empty results array — not an error, query just has no matches
error_handling.render_timeout: Retry navigate with 20s timeout, then abort
